dilemmas.scenario: A runaway trolley is heading toward five workers on the track. You can pull a lever that diverts it onto a side track, where one worker is standing. Pulling the lever will save the five but cost the one.

Same runaway trolley, same five workers. You're on a footbridge above the tracks beside a heavy stranger. The only way to stop the trolley is to push the stranger off the bridge. Their fall will stop the trolley and save the five.

You're in an overcrowded lifeboat that will sink unless one person goes overboard. An injured passenger is already unconscious. Pushing them out saves everyone else; doing nothing means the boat sinks and all of you drown.

option_deon.text: Do nothing and let the boat sink.

dilemmas.scenario: Five patients in a hospital will die today without transplants. A healthy visitor in the waiting room is a perfect match for all five. Harvesting their organs would save all five at the cost of the visitor.
You're walking past a shallow pond in your nice clothes and see a small child drowning. Wading in will ruin a few hundred dollars of clothes. No one else is around.

option_util.text: Wade in and pull the child out.

option_deon.text: Walk on and call for help.

dilemmas.scenario: A signal failure is sending a packed commuter train into a collapsed tunnel. You can remotely route it to an empty siding, but the siding runs through a maintenance bay where one worker is finishing a repair.

option_util.text: Route the train to the siding.
